description: Got a JSON at /home/user/experiments/run_047.json with our latest hyperparams — need to convert it to CSV for the tracking spreadsheet. Single flat object, no nesting, just keys and values. Drop it at /home/user/experiments/run_047.csv, header row with the keys, one data row with the values.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/experiments/run_047.json exists and contains:
    {"run_id": "047", "learning_rate": 0.001, "batch_size": 32, "epochs": 50, "dropout": 0.2, "optimizer": "adam"}
- /home/user/experiments/ is writable by the agent.
- Python 3.x available with standard library (json, csv modules). jq installed.
- /home/user/experiments/run_047.csv does not exist.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/experiments/run_047.csv exists.
- First line is a header row containing all six keys from the JSON (run_id, learning_rate, batch_size, epochs, dropout, optimizer) in any order, comma-separated.
- Second line contains the corresponding values in the same column order as the header.
- File is valid CSV parseable by Python's csv module.
- Values match the JSON exactly: run_id is "047" (string), learning_rate is 0.001, batch_size is 32, epochs is 50, dropout is 0.2, optimizer is "adam".

Invariants:
- /home/user/experiments/run_047.json unchanged (byte-identical to initial state).

Anti-shortcut guards:
- CSV must have exactly 2 lines (header + data row).
- All six fields from the source JSON must appear in the CSV.
